name: Mashed Potato Gratin with Garlic and Onions
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove the papery covering of the garlic heads, but do not peel or separate the cloves. Wrap them well in foil and place in the oven.
2. Place the onions on a double layer of foil; do not wrap them. Put both in the oven.
3. Pierce the potatoes several times with a thin skewer or fork tines. Place directly on the oven rack.
4. Bake garlic for 1 hour, and onions and potatoes for 1.5 hours at 425°F.
5. After an hour, remove the garlic heads, unwrap, and let cool for 5 minutes. Separate cloves and press them into a bowl to extract softened garlic.
6. After 1.5 hours, remove onions and potatoes. If onions are not very soft (almost collapsed), return them for a few minutes.
7. Pierce the potatoes lengthwise and crosswise with fork tines and scoop into a bowl.
8. Mash potatoes with a potato masher.
9. Cut off stem and root ends of onions; remove outer skin and first layer. Pulse in food processor until pureed.
10. Combine mashed potatoes, garlic, onion purée, 1/4 cup cream, salt, and pepper in mixer bowl.
11. Beat until smooth, then scrape into a large gratin dish. Sprinkle with cheese.
12. (Optional: Refrigerate covered for up to one day; bring to room temperature before proceeding.)
13. Reduce oven to 350°F. Pour remaining cream over the mixture.
14. Bake uncovered for 40-50 minutes, or until top is browned and bubbly, and cream has reduced to a thick sauce-like consistency. Serve with leftovers; this dish is excellent when cold.
